title: The finite mean commutes with scalar multiplication

source.citation: Lloyd N. Trefethen, Spectral Methods in MATLAB, SIAM, 2000, Ch. 3, pp. 17-28.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For a finite signal, the finite mean of a scalar multiple is the scalar multiple of the finite mean.

declarations.name: finiteDC
declarations.statement: def finiteDC {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u : ι → Real) : Real := ∑ i ∈ s, u i

declarations.name: finiteMean
declarations.statement: def finiteMean {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u : ι → Real) : Real := finiteDC s u / (s.card : Real)

declarations.name: finiteMean_smul
declarations.statement: theorem finiteMean_smul {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u : ι → Real) (c : Real) : finiteMean s (fun i => c * u i) = c * finiteMean s u

tasks.input: Translate the finite-index spectral/DC identity `finiteMean_smul` into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem finiteMean_smul {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (u : ι → Real) (c : Real) : finiteMean s (fun i => c * u i) = c * finiteMean s u
Unfold finiteMean, use finiteDC_smul, and normalize division algebra.

tasks.input: Identify the main finite-sum or mean-centering lemma needed for the proof.
